title: Neutralization Shock Detector 2.0
summary: Official F1 guidance notes that Safety Car/VSC windows change pit economics immediately because stops become cheaper.
rationale: Official F1 guidance notes that Safety Car/VSC windows change pit economics immediately because stops become cheaper. Prior research already pointed toward neutralization proxies; the stronger version is to infer hidden race control shocks from cross-sectional anomalies instead of only from single-car pace features.
implementation_hint: For each race-lap, compute robust field-level shock features: median and dispersion jumps in LapTime (s), LapTime_Delta, Position_Change, and contemporaneous PitStop rate; then interact this shock score with pit-loss and stop-window features. Validate with strict race-level splits and an embargo gap around adjacent laps.
expected_effect: Should improve detection of opportunistic cheap-stop laps that simple tyre-life heuristics miss.
risk: Rain, incidents, and testing runs can create similar signatures, so the detector may conflate different regimes.
